The completion of the road, which
will be 200 miles in length, will en-
able the Canadian Pacific to run it's
cars into Spokane and gives this city
the long prayed for terminal rates.


The articles of Incorporation pro-
vide that the new road is to be built
according to the latest and most APP
proved methods, is to be standard
guage and fully equipped to handle
freight and passenger business on a
large scale, and with every modern
convenience of comfort and safety.


1t is also asserted by railway men
that the building of the new line is
the elimination of an agreement be-
tween the Canadian Pacific and the
Southern Pacific, whereby both roads
will be enabled to compete with the
combined Great Northern and North
ern Pacific
